candidates.name: Outreach Snippet Vault
candidates.pitch: Stores categorized text snippets with variable tokens that expand via keyboard shortcut or context menu. Works on LinkedIn, Gmail, and any web form. Lightweight build targeting the personalization gap
candidates.pain: Sales reps re-type the same personalization lines for cold emails and LinkedIn messages dozens of times per week.

candidates.name: Cloud Spend Context Overlay
candidates.pitch: Embeds real-time cost estimates on the AWS/GCP console by reading resource creation events and matching against pricing APIs. Shows running cost of visible resources in a floating panel. Niche but sti
candidates.pain: DevOps engineers context-switch between AWS console tabs and cost dashboards to understand if the feature they just deployed is burning budget.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: Design Token Inspector
candidates.pitch: Hover over any element to see its computed color, typography, spacing, and shadow values as design tokens. Matches against a user-uploaded token JSON file. One-click copy as CSS variables or Figma-fri
candidates.pain: Frontend devs spend time in DevTools manually extracting hex codes and spacing values from live UI instead of matching to design specs.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: API Breaking Change Detector
candidates.pitch: Buildable by parsing OpenAPI/Swagger docs or JSON schemas and diffing endpoint signatures. Extension highlights breaking changes in-context while viewing the API reference. Competitors like Stoplight
candidates.pain: Developers waste hours manually scanning API changelogs to catch breaking changes before updating integrations.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: PR Changelog Auto-Author
candidates.pitch: Scrapes the GitHub PR diff and generates a structured changelog: added, changed, fixed, removed. Uses local LLM calls to summarize. Ships as a GitHub PR comment. One-engineer build using a lightweight
candidates.pain: Engineering managers skip PR reviews because parsing diffs takes too long, and developers dread writing description prose.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: WebSocket Frame Debugger
candidates.pitch: Intercepts WebSocket frames in DevTools and renders them as readable JSON with timing, direction, and payload size columns. Exportable as HAR-like logs. One-engineer build leveraging Chrome's webSocke
candidates.pain: Debugging WebSocket connections requires brittle manual logging or heavy desktop apps with no browser integration.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: cURL Request Cloner
candidates.pitch: Adds a one-click copy button on each Network tab request that generates a complete cURL with headers, cookies, and body. Optional flag to export as fetch() or Python requests code. Builds on Chrome's
candidates.pain: Developers manually translate browser network requests into cURL commands to reproduce bugs in terminal or share with teammates.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: NPM Dependency CVE Radar
candidates.pitch: Scans package.json files or fetches package metadata to cross-reference against the OSV database for known vulnerabilities. Shows severity badge inline next to package names. Lightweight by querying o
candidates.pain: Frontend developers ship code with outdated npm packages but have no inline warning when browsing vulnerable dependencies on npm or GitHub.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link
